---
title: "Linux — Foundations for Life Sciences Computing"
description: "Command-line foundations for bioinformatics, clinical programming, and data science."
toc-depth: 2
---

::: {.callout-important}
**Status: Coming soon** — This tutorial will follow the full StatiX documentation standard (25 sections). [Docker](docker.qmd) and [Nextflow](nextflow.qmd) are available now.
:::

## 1. Introduction

**Linux** is the operating system that powers most servers, HPC clusters, and cloud instances used in bioinformatics and pharmaceutical computing.

### Why Linux exists in your workflow

Clinical SAS/R environments may run on Windows, but **cloud pipelines**, **sequencing cores**, and **DevOps tooling** overwhelmingly run on Linux.

### What you will learn here (upcoming)

- Navigating the filesystem
- Permissions, processes, and shell scripting
- Connecting to remote servers (SSH)
- Preparing for Docker, Git, and Nextflow

[Back to home](index.qmd)
